mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 6.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate above 6.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.5 million units signals a strengthening demand for housing that boosts consumer confidence and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with housing starts above 1.5 million units are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 103 signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and increases uncertainty, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with industrial production below 103 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month increase in real GDP signal a monetary easing cycle that boosts economic growth and reduces uncertainty, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 6.5% and a 2-month decline in initial jobless claims signal a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate above 6.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.5 million units and a 2-month increase in real disposable personal income signal a strengthening demand for housing that boosts consumer confidence and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with housing starts above 1.5 million units are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 103 and a 2-month decline in the producer price index signal a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and increases uncertainty, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with industrial production below 103 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
